body:value: As per minimum norms, a Community Health Centre (CHC) is required to be manned by four Medical Specialists i.e. Surgeon, Physician, Gynecologist and Pediatrician supported by paramedical and other staff including Pharmacist / Laboratory Technician / Nursing Staff. A Pharmacist / Laboratory Technician / Nursing Staff is also available at Primary Health Centre (PHC) level. The requirement is based on norm of one Pharmacist / Laboratory Technician per each existing PHC and CHC and the requirement is based on norm of one Nursing Staff per Primary Health Centre and seven per Community Health Centre. Sanctioned and In-Position figures are as reported by the States / UTs. All India figures for Vacancy (Sanctioned In-position) and Shortfall (Requirement In-position) are the totals of State-wise Vacancy and Shortfall ignoring surplus in some States/UTs.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Rural Health Statistics
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Department of Health and Family Welfare
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Family Welfare
title: Pharmacists, Laboratory Technicians and Nursing Staff At Primary Health Centres and Community Health Centres

body:value: The data refers to target and achievement details of various components under Clean Milk Production (CMP) Scheme as on 30th September 2013. It provides details on the total number of farmers trained, number of BMC for milk Chilling (Nos), chilling capacity of BMC (LPD) and strengthening of existing lab (nos). So far under this scheme 7.03 lakh farmers have been trained and 2231 numbers of Bulk milk coolers with a total chilling capacity of 46,13,800 litre per day have been installed and 1542 existing laboratories have been strengthened.

Dairying

body:value: The data refers to information about the physical and financial progress of 5 schemes covered under NSAP in Eleventh Plan from 2007-08 to 2011-12 (Twelfth Five Year Plan document). At present NSAP is an integration of the social security program that are: (i) Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS); (ii) Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS); (iii) Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS); (vi) National Family Benefit Scheme (NFBS); and (v) Annapurna Scheme. IGNOAPS is the largest scheme within NSAP. In 201112, 82 per cent of the total NSAP expenditure was on IGNOAPS followed by the IGNWPS at 9.7 per cent.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Rural Development
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Urban
Rural
Development
title: Physical And Financial Progress of NSAP Components

body:value: JNNURM (Jawaharlal Nehru National Urban Renewal Mission) has four components viz Urban Infrastructure and Governance (UIG) and Urban Infrastructure Development Scheme for Small and Medium Towns (UIDSSMT) (implemented by M/o UD) and Basic Services to the Urban Poor (BSUP) and Integrated Housing and Slum Development Programme (IHSDP) (implemented by M/o HUPA). In the projects, sanctioned under JNNURM, the per cent of assistance varies among different components. For example, under BSUP, 50% of project is covered by Central Govt. whereas under IHSDP/UIDSSMT, 80% cost is covered.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Urban Development
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Urban
Development
title: Physical And Financial Progress Under JNNURM

body:value: Physical and Financial Progress of Pardhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojna (PMGSY), Swarnajayanti Gram Swarozgar Yojana(SGSY) and Bharat Nirman have been furnished in the data. The Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana or PMGSY is a nationwide plan in India to provide good all-weather road connectivity to unconnected villages. Swarnajayanti Gram Swarojgar Yojana (SGSY) is an initiative launched by the Government of India to provide sustainable income to poor people living in rural areas of the country. Bharat Nirman is a time-bound business plan for action in rural infrastructure. Under Bharat Nirman, action is proposed in the areas of irrigation, rural housing, rural water supply, rural electrification and rural telecommunication connectivity.

field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation

body:value: The data refers to information on Physical Performance (Target and Achievement) of New Connectivity/Upgradation of Roads in Sample Blocks during 2005-06 to 2006-07. It provides details such as Habitations with 1000+ Population, Habitations with 500+ Population, Habitations Covered, Length (KM), Targets, Achievements for the selected sample states. The outcome is based on the sample survey undertaken by Programme Evaluation Organisation (PEO), Planning commission which covered 14 districts, 27 blocks, 138 roads, 138 habitations and 1380 beneficiary households spread over 7 states of India for 'Evaluation Study on Rural Roads component of Bharat Nirman, 2010'. The reference period for the study was 2005-06 to 2006-07.

title: Physical Performance of Rural Road Scheme (Upgradation) - Based on Sample Survey

body:value: The data refers to the physical parameters of reported SRTUs in India in terms of Average Fleet Held and Average Fleet Operated, Average Age of Fleet, Percentage of Overage Fleet to Total Fleet, Revenue Earning kilometre, Staff Strength, Fuel Efficiency, Passenger kilometres Offered, Passenger kilometre Performed, Passenger Carried and some accident related parameters like Number of Accidents and Number of Fatal Accidents of the reporting SRTUs. While, Average Fleet Held and Average Fleet Operated by various SRTUs may be used in order to define fleet utilisation, staff strength may be useful to calculate staff productivity. The Occupancy Ratio is defined as the Passenger kilometres Performed to Passenger kilometres offered.

title: Physical Performance of SRTUs

body:value: The data refers to information about Plan wise allocation and actual expenditure under IDDP (Intensive Dairy Development Programme) Scheme from Eighth Plan to Eleventh. IDDP scheme was launched in 1993-94 on 100% grant-in-aid basis. The main objectives of the scheme are developing milch cattle, increasing milk production by providing technical input services, creating infrastructure to improve procurement, processing and marketing of milk in a cost effective manner, ensuring remunerative prices to the milk producers, by strengthening dairy cooperative societies at village level, generate additional employment opportunities, improving social, nutritional and economic status of residents of comparatively disadvantaged areas. The funds are released directly to the implementing agency i.e State Dairy Federation/District Milk Union. The Scheme is being continued during the 11th plan with a total plan outlay of Rs.275.00 Crore as merged scheme together with Clean Milk Production' Scheme.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Dairying Scheme
field_ministry_department:name: Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying
Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying
field_sector:name: Agriculture
Dairying
title: Plan allocation and Actual Expenditure under IDDP Scheme

body:value: The data refers to information about Plan wise allocation and actual expenditure under Assistance to Cooperatives (A to C) Scheme during Ninth, Tenth and Eleventh Plan.
